target.title: fix: fallback when generated media handoff locks
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the existing #85489 active-wake generated-media fallback intact.
- Narrow the thrown-error fallback to the session write-lock/no-response shape only.
- Preserve the visible failure contract for generic requester-agent handoff errors so they do not direct-send generated media after an unknown side effect.

## Why this is narrow

This does not change the normal active-session success path, dormant requester behavior, or generic requester-agent handoff failures. The fallback is only attempted when all of these are true:

- the completion is for an agent-mediated generated-media tool,
- expected media URLs are present,
- the active requester wake has already failed,
- the requester-agent handoff throws before producing a response,
- the thrown error is recognized as `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`, `OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_TIMEOUT`, or the serialized `session file locked` message,
- the existing direct media delivery target is available.

This specifically covers the observed Spartacus failure shape: generation succeeded, active wake failed with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, the requester-agent handoff then hit a session write lock, and the generated image was reported as failed instead of being delivered.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: active wake failure followed by a requester-agent session write-lock handoff error now delivers the generated media through the existing idempotent `generated-media-direct` fallback. Generic requester-agent handoff errors still return a visible failed delivery record and do not direct-send media.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout, production `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` path via the repo Vitest wrapper with controlled gateway and queue boundaries. This is source-level proof for the exact failure shape, not qa-channel/live proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix:

```text
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure
✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks
✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  152 passed (152)
```

Observed result after fix: the lock-shaped handoff error sends exactly `/tmp/generated-locked.png` with idempotency key `announce-channel-media-handoff-locked:generated-media-direct`; the generic-error case returns `delivered: false`, `path: direct`, and does not call `sendMessage`.

What was not tested: qa-channel/live reproduction of the exact active-wake-failed plus session-write-lock handoff race. Existing qa-channel media scenarios cover generated-media delivery, but do not currently provide a harness hook for this lock contention shape.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 152 passed (152)`.
- `pnpm format:fix -- src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Known baseline issue

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:core` previously failed on unchanged upstream/main code:

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.abort-settle-timeout.ts(4,3): error TS2322: Type 'ProcessEnv' is not assignable to type 'Pick<ProcessEnv, "OPENCLAW_EMBEDDED_ABORT_SETTLE_TIMEOUT_MS" | "OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST">'.
```

That file is untouched by this PR; the PR diff is limited to `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` and `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
